title: anthemis arvensis subsp. cyllenea
intro: Anthemis arvensis subsp. cyllenea, also known as mountain mayweed, is a subspecies of the Anthemis arvensis plant, which is a member of the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Greece and Turkey. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of mountain mayweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions. Mountain mayweed can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of essential oils, which can be applied topically or used in aromatherapy.
